statusLine: European fuel security remains under strain with 15 of 27 countries showing critical stock levels in at least one fuel type, despite overall EU averages above mandatory minimums.
fullAnalysis:
European fuel security faces significant pressure across multiple countries, with 15 of 27 EU member states recording critical stock levels in at least one fuel category. Romania presents the most severe situation with all three fuel types in critical status: petrol at just 40.4 days, diesel at 30.2 days, and jet fuel at 76.4 days. Poland and Luxembourg also show critical levels across all fuel categories, while major economies including Germany, France, Italy, and Spain have critical shortages in diesel or jet fuel despite adequate petrol reserves.

Jet fuel emerges as the most vulnerable fuel type, with 16 of 27 countries below the 90-day mandatory minimum. This widespread aviation fuel shortage affects key economies including Germany (64.7 days), France (67.5 days), and the Netherlands (51.1 days). Diesel stocks show concerning patterns with 12 countries in critical status, including Germany at just 64.3 days, highlighting supply chain vulnerabilities in this essential transport and heating fuel.

The current situation reflects ongoing strain from Middle East supply disruptions and strategic reserve releases implemented earlier this year. Brent crude oil prices have surged 27.9% since late February, reaching $92.67 per barrel, contributing to elevated fuel costs across the region. While the EU's overall averages of 113 days for petrol and 102.1 days for diesel provide some buffer above the 90-day minimum requirement, the uneven distribution of reserves across member states creates significant regional vulnerabilities that require close monitoring.
keyPoints: 15 of 27 EU countries show critical stock levels in at least one fuel type, with Romania facing the most severe shortages across all categories
Jet fuel represents the greatest vulnerability with 16 countries below the 90-day minimum, including major economies like Germany and France
Diesel supplies are under pressure in 12 countries, with Germany's reserves critically low at just 64.3 days
Oil prices have surged 27.9% since late February disruptions, reaching $92.67 per barrel and contributing to elevated fuel costs
Despite overall EU averages meeting mandatory minimums, uneven distribution creates significant regional supply vulnerabilities
